concerns.analysis: The output contains a fabricated link on page 6 that does not exist in the source document. The text 'support the Carolina heelsplit mussel ” According to the rep' is wrapped in a link to an email address ('mailto:aeckardt@wildlandseng.com'), which has no basis in that paragraph in the original PDF.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Text on page 6: 'support the Carolina heelsplitter (Lasmigona decorata), a federally and state endangered freshwater mussel.” According to the report' (no hyperlink present).
concerns.output_quote: {"type": "link", "text": "support the Carolina heelsplit mussel ” According to the rep", "url": "mailto:aeckardt@wildlandseng.com"}

concerns.analysis: Multiple highly meaningful maps from the source PDF are missing from the HTML output. Specifically, the maps on pages 13, 14, 15, and 20 failed to render as `` elements and were instead replaced by plain text placeholders formatted as `[Image: ]`. Because these are critical visual components containing geographic and project data that cannot be fully conveyed by text alone, this is a severe visual fidelity failure.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Full-page maps on pages 13, 14, 15, and 20 (e.g., "Project Vicinity Map")
concerns.output_quote: [Image: A project vicinity map for the Running Dog Mitigation Site, located east of Charlotte, North Carolina. The map shows major highways...]

concerns.description: Missing images: 26 of 43 source image(s) not present in output
concerns.analysis: image_count_source=43, html_img_count=17, image_count_ratio=1.0930232558139534. Over half of source images are missing from the output.

The source PDF contains a Table of Contents that relies on page numbers and dotted leaders. The extraction pipeline retained this TOC as plain text paragraphs, preserving the meaningless page numbers and dotted leaders instead of converting them to functional navigation links or removing them, which violates the strict requirement regarding dead TOCs.

concerns.description: Missing table(s): source PDF has tables not found in output on p23(source:1,output:0), p24(source:1,output:0), p25(source:1,output:0), p27(source:1,output:0), p28(source:1,output:0)
concerns.analysis: source_tables=26, output_tables=28. Pages with missing tables: p23(source:1,output:0), p24(source:1,output:0), p25(source:1,output:0), p27(source:1,output:0), p28(source:1,output:0)

concerns.description: Table split across page break(s): 5 table(s) with matching structure across consecutive pages
concerns.analysis: split_table_count=5. Examples: pages 34-35: 7 cols (plot, scientific name...); pages 35-36: 7 cols (plot, scientific name...); pages 36-37: 7 cols (plot, scientific name...). Tables spanning multiple pages were extracted as separate table elements. In HTML, these should be one continuous table to maintain accessibility and reading order.

concerns.description: Table caption coded as header row: 3 table(s) have a single spanning first row that is likely a caption/title
concerns.analysis: caption_as_header=3. Examples: p16: "TOTAL AREA OF BUFFER MITIGATION (TABM)"; p18: "Project Information"; p33: "Vegetation Performance Standards Summary Table". First row spans all columns — should be a <caption> element, not a header row.

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (1 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=1. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Degraded image quality: 28 image(s) used fallback rendering instead of direct extraction
concerns.analysis: fallback_rendered_images=28. Examples: p23: "Three aerial photographs show a green vegetated sw"; p23: "Three aerial photographs show a vegetated drainage"; p23: "Three aerial photographs display a vegetated chann". These images were not directly extracted from the PDF — instead, an approximate page region was rendered as a PNG. Fallback images may be cropped incorrectly, show the wrong content, or miss meaningful visual elements.

concerns.description:
Header/footer elements retained in output (26): Wildlands Engineering, Inc. • phone 704-332-7754 • fax 704-3; Wildlands Engineering, Inc. • phone 704-332-7754 • fax 704-3; Running Dog Mitigation Site
Monitoring Year 1 Annual Report
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=26, pages_affected=20/42. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: 2 bare URL(s) in text not converted to hyperlinks: http://cvs.bio.unc.edu/protocol/cvs-eep-protocol-v4.2-lev1-2.pdf; https://files.nc.gov/ncdeq/Mitigation%20Services/Watershed_Planning/Yadkin_River
concerns.analysis: bare_url_count=2. URLs appear as plain text instead of clickable links. Per NCDIT criteria, link text not wrapped in href is a major defect.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 5 table(s) have a data row marked as header: "Appendix 1", "Yadkin - Goose Creek", "Activity or Report"; 7 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th=0, datarow_as_header=5, no_th_tables=7. Tables use <th> for data values, have a data row mismarked as a header row, or lack <th> elements entirely. Screen readers rely on <th> to announce column/row context.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 32 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (32 truncated mid-sentence) — e.g. p1: "...ip with a narrow stream running through its center"; p1: "...dark stripe across its eye clings to a thick green"; p1: "... large "NC" letters, with a green 'N' and blue 'C'"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=32 (truncated=32, placeholder=0, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

concerns.description: Table row count mismatch: p16(source:11rows,output:3rows), p16(source:12rows,output:3rows), p31(source:26rows,output:6rows), p32(source:26rows,output:6rows)
concerns.analysis: Matched tables have >20% row count difference (delta >=3). Pages: p16(source:11rows,output:3rows), p16(source:12rows,output:3rows), p31(source:26rows,output:6rows), p32(source:26rows,output:6rows)

concerns.description: Table column count mismatch: p16(source:17cols,output:2cols), p16(source:13cols,output:17cols), p31(source:17cols,output:2cols), p32(source:17cols,output:2cols), p42(source:11cols,output:4cols)
concerns.analysis: Matched tables have different column counts. Pages: p16(source:17cols,output:2cols), p16(source:13cols,output:17cols), p31(source:17cols,output:2cols), p32(source:17cols,output:2cols), p42(source:11cols,output:4cols)

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 2 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=2, examples=['mailto:aeckardt@wildlandseng.com', 'mailto:aeckardt@wildlandseng.com']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.
